name: John Cameron Mitchell & Friends
description: John Cameron Mitchell returns to The Town Hall for Pride 2026 with some friends in town! Mitchell brings his anarchic, downtown sensibilities to the Hall that has been a home over his career with the help of longtime Hedwig collaborators Justin Craig (Music Director of Stereophonic) and international cabaret star Amber Martin (Scissor Sisters). Expect a musical and theatrical extravaganza that can only come from the mind behind Hedwig and the Angry Inch and Short Bus.

name: Phantom Of The Opera (Entry requires a valid photo ID. All guests must be 13+. Any guest under 16 must be accompanied by an adult)
ANDY FRASCO & THE U.N. CONCERT CRUISE
description: ANDY FRASCO & The U.N. RETURNS TO NEW YORK CITY AND TAKES THE MUSIC TO THE WATER! WIth Special Guest Caleb Hawley!Love, Youre Just Too Expensive.. But concert cruise tickets are priced just right! This is a 3 hour concert cruise bringing you all around NY Harbor for the best views in and of New York City the Greatest City in The World!! Its Just A Good Ole Time as we will get up close and personal with the Statue of Liberty, take in the iconic skyline, cruise under the Brooklyn, Manhattan and Williamsburg Bridges and explore the East and Hudson Rivers! Get ready for a Road Life Revival.. On a boat!!! Experience the Happy Bastards that comprise Andy Frasco On and be sure to Wash, Rinse, Repeat until the tickets are locked in!!!This cruise is aboard THE LIBERTY BELLE and will depart from Pier 36 299 South Street. We will start boarding at 6:00 pm and departing promptly at 7pm, returning to the dock right around 10pm! 21+We are *not* able to allow refunds if you miss the boat. So dont be late it sure aint pretty to be standin on the dock, watching the party sailing away! Be an LOptomist and youll never feel Growing Pains! There are bars on board so you have options if you groove yourself into a thirst...And, there will also be food available for purchaseCash and Cards accepted! ATM on Board! If you have any questions about the show or your purchase, please contact Sancho Dominguez at sancho@rocksoff.com or check out our FAQ page on www.rocksoff.com Please note that all shows are 21 and over and ID will be required upon boarding the boat. LAST CALL for the boats is 5 minutes before departure After this time your tickets may be forfeited and you may not be able to board the boat, so *please arrive early*

Rock Never Dies
description: Fall in Love with Rock All Over AgainInside the legendary Hard Rock Cafe in Times Square, discover an experience where music isnt just heard but felt.Rock Never Dies is a one-of-a-kind immersive theatrical event from American Immersion Theater that is part concert, part love story, and fully a magical journey. This groundbreaking production invites you to dine, drink, and fall headfirst into the soul of rock and roll.Enjoy a full three-course meal inside the Magical Mystery Museum of Hard Rock, a place where music has power, memories come alive, and one life is changed forever every night. Its an experience that inspires you to keep dreaming and march to the beat of your own drum.At the heart of the story is Caroline, a young artist whos lost hope and heart. As legendary music icons come to life around her, shes forced to confront love, loss, and the courage to take the stage again. Along the way, you wont just watch the story, but be part of it.Created by award-winning, immersive visionary Scott Cramton, often called the Willy Wonka of Immersive Theater, this joyful, high-energy spectacle blends iconic rock anthems with romance, comedy, and electrifying audience interaction.Whether youre on a date, celebrating something special, or just chasing that feeling music used to give you, this is your night.All it takesis one song,one moment,and a second chanceto change everything.

name: Lucy Yeghiazaryan Sextet
description: Lucy Yeghiazaryan is an American Armenian vocalist who has become a leading voice in American straight-ahead jazz by bringing the real richness of her heritage together with her love for the ultimate American artform. She is a recipient of grants from the Doris Duke Foundation, Chamber Music America and the New York Foundation for the Arts. She has shared the stage with Harold Mabern, Houston Person, Grant Stewart, Terell Stafford, Bernadette Peters, Arto Tuncboyaijian and many more. She regularly works with the Emmet Cohen trio and can be found performing in and outside of The United States with regular appearances at Jazz at Lincoln Center, Birdland, Mezzrow, Symphony Space and more. She was a top ten finalist in the 2015 Thelonious Monk Competition and holds a degree in World History in addition to being a skilled classical violinist. Her debut album, Blue Heaven, was released in 2019 on Cellar Live Records and was followed by the album In Her Words on La_Reserve Records in 2021 which was sponsored by The Womens Fund of NYFA. Her third album, Lonely House will be released in late 2022.
